preSessionState.CLAUDE.md:
# Project Guidelines

## Architecture
- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
- All business logic lives in the service layer

## Coding Conventions
- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
- Prefer async/await over raw Promises

## Testing
- Tests use vitest
- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method

## File Organization
- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
- src/services/ — Business logic layer
- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
- src/config/ — Configuration files
- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure

## Git Practices
- Commit atomically per logical change
- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
- Run tests before committing

{"entry_type":"status","summary":"Agent 1: Notification system foundation complete — event-driven architecture via EventBus + NotificationAdapter pattern","detail":"Implemented:
1. ARCHITECTURE: Chose event-driven (EventBus) over direct calls. All notifications flow through EventBus.emit() → NotificationService → NotificationAdapter.
2. CORE TYPES: src/models/notification.ts — NotificationType, NotificationChannel, NotificationPayload, NotificationResult.
3. ADAPTER INTERFACE: src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts — NotificationAdapter interface (channel, send method).
4. EMAIL ADAPTER: src/adapters/email.adapter.ts — EmailAdapter implements NotificationAdapter with in-memory stub.
5. NOTIFICATION SERVICE: Rewritten src/services/notification.service.ts — subscribes to EventBus, delegates to adapters via registerAdapter().
6. UNIFIED PATTERN: Removed CallbackRegistry usage from OrderService.updateOrderStatus — now emits via EventBus like all other events.
7. Updated tests and exports. All 69 tests pass, clean compile.

CONSTRAINTS FOR OTHER DEVELOPERS:
- All adapters MUST implement NotificationAdapter interface
- All notifications MUST go through EventBus.emit() — never call adapters directly
- Each NotificationChannel maps to exactly one adapter
- NotificationService is the sole coordinator between events and adapters
- Adapters handle delivery only — they must NOT subscribe to EventBus directly","tags":["architecture","notification","event-driven"],"scope":"src/","agent_id":"agent-1","id":"01KN0YE3FN00WX8A50YA07KGBP","timestamp":"2026-03-31T03:19:14.677Z"}

{"entry_type":"status","summary":"Agent 2: Email adapter fully implemented — template rendering, recipient resolution, pluggable transport. 26 new tests, all 95 pass.","detail":"Implemented:
1. TEMPLATE RENDERING: Built-in EmailTemplate objects for all 4 notification types (order-confirmation, order-status-update, welcome, user-updated). Each renders both HTML and plain text. Default fallback template for unknown types. HTML output uses escapeHtml to prevent XSS.
2. RECIPIENT RESOLUTION: Pluggable RecipientResolver function. Default resolver passes through email addresses (containing @) and rejects raw IDs. Custom resolvers can map user IDs to emails.
3. TRANSPORT ABSTRACTION: EmailTransport interface for delivery. InMemoryTransport default for testing. Production use plugs in SES/SendGrid/SMTP implementations.
4. CONFIGURATION: EmailAdapterConfig allows overriding transport, resolver, and templates at construction. Zero-arg construction still works for simple cases.
5. TESTS: 26 new tests in tests/adapters/email.adapter.test.ts covering: send success/failure, all 4 template types, recipient resolution (direct email, user ID, custom resolver, null resolver), transport errors (failure result, thrown Error, thrown non-Error), custom template overrides, InMemoryTransport, escapeHtml, built-in template coverage.
6. Updated tests/services/notification.service.test.ts to use InMemoryTransport and a test RecipientResolver.

CONSTRAINTS FOR OTHER DEVELOPERS:
- EmailAdapter constructor accepts optional EmailAdapterConfig
- Custom transports must implement EmailTransport interface
- Custom templates must implement EmailTemplate (renderHtml + renderText)
- RecipientResolver returns null for unresolvable recipients, causing a graceful failure result
- Always use escapeHtml for user-provided content in HTML templates","tags":["implementation","email","adapters","testing"],"scope":"src/adapters/","agent_id":"agent-2","id":"01KN0YNBJPJNAQHR01XRFF4BHD","timestamp":"2026-03-31T03:23:12.342Z"}
{"entry_type":"decision","summary":"User notification preferences use category-based subscriptions with email frequency control, stored via repository pattern","detail":"Design preferences around: (1) NotificationCategory groupings (order-updates, account) that map from NotificationType — this is more user-friendly than per-type toggles and allows new notification types to be added to existing categories. (2) EmailFrequency (immediate, daily-digest, weekly-digest, none) for controlling delivery cadence. (3) A mapping from NotificationType to NotificationCategory so the notification service can look up the category for any notification type and check if the user is subscribed. (4) Default preferences opt users into all categories with immediate delivery — safe default. (5) PreferencesRepository follows the existing BaseRepository pattern. (6) PreferencesService provides CRUD + a shouldSendNotification() method that the NotificationService calls before sending. (7) NotificationService receives an optional PreferencesService via a setter — keeps backward compatibility and doesn't break existing tests.","tags":["architecture"],"scope":"src/models/user-preferences.ts","agent_id":"agent-3","id":"01KN0YSCD060RHJ7TJKVET5DJ9","timestamp":"2026-03-31T03:25:24.256Z"}

postSessionState.CLAUDE.md:
# Project Guidelines

## Architecture
- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
- All business logic lives in the service layer

## Coding Conventions
- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
- Prefer async/await over raw Promises

## Testing
- Tests use vitest
- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method

## File Organization
- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
- src/services/ — Business logic layer
- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
- src/config/ — Configuration files
- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure

## Git Practices
- Commit atomically per logical change
- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
- Run tests before committing

{"entry_type":"decision","summary":"Notification system uses event-driven architecture via EventBus, not direct calls","detail":"EventBus is already the dominant pattern — OrderService, UserService both emit events through it. It provides: (1) loose coupling between producers and consumers, (2) an event log for debugging/testing, (3) typed events via TypeScript discriminated unions, (4) easy extensibility — new notification channels just subscribe to existing events. The CallbackRegistry pattern will be eliminated in favor of routing all events through EventBus.","tags":["architecture"],"scope":"src/services/notification.service.ts","agent_id":"agent-1","id":"01KN0Y9GW7BVNBX9ANNKSWHEP6","timestamp":"2026-03-31T03:16:44.551Z"}

{"entry_type":"status","summary":"Agent 1: Notification system foundation complete — event-driven architecture via EventBus + NotificationAdapter pattern","detail":"Implemented:
1. ARCHITECTURE: Chose event-driven (EventBus) over direct calls. All notifications flow through EventBus.emit() → NotificationService → NotificationAdapter.
2. CORE TYPES: src/models/notification.ts — NotificationType, NotificationChannel, NotificationPayload, NotificationResult.
3. ADAPTER INTERFACE: src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts — NotificationAdapter interface (channel, send method).
4. EMAIL ADAPTER: src/adapters/email.adapter.ts — EmailAdapter implements NotificationAdapter with in-memory stub.
5. NOTIFICATION SERVICE: Rewritten src/services/notification.service.ts — subscribes to EventBus, delegates to adapters via registerAdapter().
6. UNIFIED PATTERN: Removed CallbackRegistry usage from OrderService.updateOrderStatus — now emits via EventBus like all other events.
7. Updated tests and exports. All 69 tests pass, clean compile.

CONSTRAINTS FOR OTHER DEVELOPERS:
- All adapters MUST implement NotificationAdapter interface
- All notifications MUST go through EventBus.emit() — never call adapters directly
- Each NotificationChannel maps to exactly one adapter
- NotificationService is the sole coordinator between events and adapters
- Adapters handle delivery only — they must NOT subscribe to EventBus directly","tags":["architecture","notification","event-driven"],"scope":"src/","agent_id":"agent-1","id":"01KN0YE3FN00WX8A50YA07KGBP","timestamp":"2026-03-31T03:19:14.677Z"}

{"entry_type":"decision","summary":"Email adapter uses pluggable transport, recipient resolver, and per-type template rendering","detail":"Three extension points make the adapter production-ready without changing the NotificationAdapter contract: (1) EmailTransport interface abstracts delivery (SES, SendGrid, SMTP) — defaults to InMemoryTransport for testing. (2) RecipientResolver function resolves user IDs to email addresses, enabling the notification service to pass raw user IDs. (3) Per-NotificationType templates with HTML and text renderers, with built-in defaults and override support. HTML output uses escapeHtml to prevent XSS. All three are optional constructor config — zero-arg construction still works for simple testing.","tags":["implementation"],"scope":"src/adapters/email.adapter.ts","agent_id":"agent-2","id":"01KN0YMTC0DK1M1NZR6MGY5BR9","timestamp":"2026-03-31T03:22:54.720Z"}

{"entry_type":"status","summary":"Agent 2: Email adapter fully implemented — template rendering, recipient resolution, pluggable transport. 26 new tests, all 95 pass.","detail":"Implemented:
1. TEMPLATE RENDERING: Built-in EmailTemplate objects for all 4 notification types (order-confirmation, order-status-update, welcome, user-updated). Each renders both HTML and plain text. Default fallback template for unknown types. HTML output uses escapeHtml to prevent XSS.
2. RECIPIENT RESOLUTION: Pluggable RecipientResolver function. Default resolver passes through email addresses (containing @) and rejects raw IDs. Custom resolvers can map user IDs to emails.
3. TRANSPORT ABSTRACTION: EmailTransport interface for delivery. InMemoryTransport default for testing. Production use plugs in SES/SendGrid/SMTP implementations.
4. CONFIGURATION: EmailAdapterConfig allows overriding transport, resolver, and templates at construction. Zero-arg construction still works for simple cases.
5. TESTS: 26 new tests in tests/adapters/email.adapter.test.ts covering: send success/failure, all 4 template types, recipient resolution (direct email, user ID, custom resolver, null resolver), transport errors (failure result, thrown Error, thrown non-Error), custom template overrides, InMemoryTransport, escapeHtml, built-in template coverage.
6. Updated tests/services/notification.service.test.ts to use InMemoryTransport and a test RecipientResolver.

CONSTRAINTS FOR OTHER DEVELOPERS:
- EmailAdapter constructor accepts optional EmailAdapterConfig
- Custom transports must implement EmailTransport interface
- Custom templates must implement EmailTemplate (renderHtml + renderText)
- RecipientResolver returns null for unresolvable recipients, causing a graceful failure result
- Always use escapeHtml for user-provided content in HTML templates","tags":["implementation","email","adapters","testing"],"scope":"src/adapters/","agent_id":"agent-2","id":"01KN0YNBJPJNAQHR01XRFF4BHD","timestamp":"2026-03-31T03:23:12.342Z"}
{"entry_type":"decision","summary":"User notification preferences use category-based subscriptions with email frequency control, stored via repository pattern","detail":"Design preferences around: (1) NotificationCategory groupings (order-updates, account) that map from NotificationType — this is more user-friendly than per-type toggles and allows new notification types to be added to existing categories. (2) EmailFrequency (immediate, daily-digest, weekly-digest, none) for controlling delivery cadence. (3) A mapping from NotificationType to NotificationCategory so the notification service can look up the category for any notification type and check if the user is subscribed. (4) Default preferences opt users into all categories with immediate delivery — safe default. (5) PreferencesRepository follows the existing BaseRepository pattern. (6) PreferencesService provides CRUD + a shouldSendNotification() method that the NotificationService calls before sending. (7) NotificationService receives an optional PreferencesService via a setter — keeps backward compatibility and doesn't break existing tests.","tags":["architecture"],"scope":"src/models/user-preferences.ts","agent_id":"agent-3","id":"01KN0YSCD060RHJ7TJKVET5DJ9","timestamp":"2026-03-31T03:25:24.256Z"}

{"entry_type":"status","summary":"Agent 3: User notification preferences complete — model, repository, service, notification integration, 22 new tests, all 117 pass.","detail":"Implemented:
1. MODEL: src/models/user-preferences.ts — NotificationCategory ('order-updates', 'account'), EmailFrequency ('immediate', 'daily-digest', 'weekly-digest', 'none'), EmailPreferences (enabled, frequency, subscribedCategories), UserPreferences, notificationTypeToCategory mapping, defaults.
2. REPOSITORY: src/repositories/preferences.repository.ts — PreferencesRepository extends BaseRepository with findByUserId(). Stores email prefs as flattened fields with JSON-serialized categories.
3. SERVICE: src/services/preferences.service.ts — PreferencesService with getPreferences(), createPreferences(), updatePreferences(), deletePreferences(), shouldSendEmail(userId, notificationType).
4. INTEGRATION: NotificationService.setPreferencesService() — optional setter maintains backward compatibility. sendNotification() checks preferences before delegating to adapter. Each event handler passes userId.
5. TESTS: 17 new tests in tests/services/preferences.service.test.ts (CRUD, defaults, shouldSendEmail with various preference combinations). 5 new tests in tests/services/notification.service.test.ts (preferences integration: default send, disabled email, unsubscribed category, subscribed category, frequency none).
6. EXPORTS: All new types, classes, and functions exported from src/index.ts.

CONSTRAINTS FOR OTHER DEVELOPERS:
- PreferencesService is optional on NotificationService — when not set, all notifications are sent (backward compatible)
- shouldSendEmail() returns true when no preferences exist for a user (safe default)
- NotificationCategory groups NotificationType — add new types to notificationTypeToCategory mapping
- Email is the only channel with preferences for now — UserPreferences can be extended with additional channel settings later","tags":["preferences","notification","email"],"scope":"src/","agent_id":"agent-3","id":"01KN0YZ23PNDE46HGKVV7VXGS1","timestamp":"2026-03-31T03:28:30.326Z"}

{"entry_type":"status","summary":"Agent 4: Fixed pagination off-by-one bug — offset was `(page-1)*pageSize - 1` instead of `(page-1)*pageSize`. Added 3 regression tests. All 120 tests pass.","detail":"Bug: src/utils/pagination.ts line 45 had `const offset = page === 1 ? 0 : (page - 1) * pageSize - 1`. The `- 1` caused page 2+ to start one item too early, producing duplicates between pages. Fix: simplified to `const offset = (page - 1) * pageSize`. Added 3 regression tests: (1) no duplicates between page 1 and 2, (2) correct items on page 2, (3) all items covered across all pages with no gaps or duplicates.","tags":["bugfix","pagination"],"scope":"src/utils/pagination.ts","agent_id":"agent-4","id":"01KN0Z1455Y3BQXKBNH09P9YZ2","timestamp":"2026-03-31T03:29:37.957Z"}
